response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship, but their background is fundamentally misaligned with this role. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, GPU or accelerator architecture work, deep-learning workload performance analysis, or hardware-software co-design. The closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang services and some C/C++ tooling work, which falls far short of principal-level hardware performance architecture for AI accelerators.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) of shipping software across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput microservices in DLang)
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience whatsoever
No deep-learning training/inference optimization, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture — a principal hardware IC role would be a drastic domain jump

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture background
General seniority and low-latency systems exposure
